description: Zwartruggens Commando mobilised at Zwartruggens on declaration of war, October 1899, forming part of the Boer republican forces.
source: angloboerwar.com unit registry; Handbook of the Boer War

description: The Natal Carbineers, the premier Natal volunteer cavalry regiment, mobilised in October 1899 and served throughout the Natal campaign, the siege of Ladysmith, and subsequent operations in the Transvaal.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook

description: The Natal Police served throughout the Natal campaign, providing scouting, intelligence and garrison duties alongside the imperial forces; they suffered heavily in the early battles and the siege of Ladysmith.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Baden-Powell's ~1,200-man garrison withstood 217 days of Boer siege at Mafeking, 13 Oct 1899 – 17 May 1900. Garrison used innovative tactics and sorties; relief by Mahon and Plumer triggered Empire-wide celebrations.
source: Conan Doyle ch.23; Handbook ch.VIII

description: General Piet Cronje initially commanded the Boer besieging force at Mafeking, handing command to Snyman; approximately 6,000 Boers invested the town from October 1899 to May 1900.
source: Handbook ch.VIII

description: Kekewich's ~4,000-strong garrison (incl. 2,000 armed civilians) held Kimberley 15 Oct 1899 – 15 Feb 1900. Cecil Rhodes's pressure on the commander complicated defence; French's cavalry dash finally broke the siege.
source: Handbook ch.IX; Conan Doyle ch.13

description: OFS commandos under Wessels and Du Toit invested Kimberley from October 1899; the besieging force intermittently bombarded the town but lacked the numbers to storm it and was drawn away by Roberts's advance.
source: Handbook ch.IX

description: French and Hamilton routed Kock's commando at Elandslaagte 21 Oct 1899. Gordon Highlanders and Imperial Light Horse broke the Boer position; 5th Lancers pursued. One of few clear British set-piece victories early in the war; General Kock mortally wounded.
source: Conan Doyle ch.7; Handbook ch.VI

description: The Royal Canadian Regiment of Infantry (2nd Bn, Special Service Battalion) sailed from Canada and arrived at Cape Town on 9 November 1899. They assembled at Cape Town before moving by rail to join Roberts's force at the Modder River area. They fought at Sunnyside/Douglas (Jan 1 1900) with Pilcher's column and at Paardeberg (Feb 17-27 1900) as part of Roberts's main flanking force.
source: Handbook ch.XII; Conan Doyle ch.16
note: date_start corrected from 1899-11-01 to 1899-11-09 (Cape Town arrival); action_place corrected from Bloemfontein (wrong — Bloemfontein fell Mar 13 1900) to Cape Town. RCR then moved to Modder River/Douglas before Paardeberg.

description: New Zealand's 1st Contingent of 215 mounted riflemen arrived in South Africa in late 1899, serving first in the Cape Colony and then in the OFS advance under Roberts.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook

description: The Cape Mounted Rifles, the oldest colonial unit in South Africa, served throughout the war in the Cape Colony and eastern districts, including the pursuit of Scheepers, Kritzinger, and Lotter commandos.
source: angloboerwar.com; Conan Doyle ch.32

description: Imperial Light Infantry, raised in Natal from uitlander refugees in late 1899, served in the Natal campaign and subsequent operations; distinct from the Imperial Light Horse.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Natal Mounted Rifles served throughout the Natal campaign from the opening battles at Talana Hill and Elandslaagte through the siege of Ladysmith and subsequent operations.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook

description: The Rhodesia Regiment under Plumer operated from Bulawayo, covering the western Transvaal, relieving Mafeking (May 1900), and participating in columns against the Boers in the northern Transvaal.
source: Handbook ch.VIII; angloboerwar.com

description: Thorneycroft's Mounted Infantry, raised in Natal in early 1900, served throughout the Natal campaign including the storming of Spion Kop where Thorneycroft commanded the summit.
source: Handbook ch.X; Conan Doyle ch.12

description: Kitchener's Fighting Scouts (also known as Rimington's Guides or Tigers) were a South African irregular unit specialising in reconnaissance and pursuit, serving extensively in the ORC and Transvaal guerrilla campaigns.
source: angloboerwar.com; Conan Doyle ch.36

description: South African Light Horse, formed from refugees from the Transvaal goldfields, served throughout the Natal campaign including the Tugela crossings and then in the Transvaal guerrilla phase under Byng.
source: angloboerwar.com; Conan Doyle ch.36

description: Lovat's Scouts, Highland deer-stalkers raised by Lord Lovat, arrived in 1900 and served as specialist scouts and marksmen in the Cape Colony during the guerrilla phase.
source: angloboerwar.com; Conan Doyle

description: Umvoti Mounted Rifles, a Natal volunteer regiment, served in the Natal campaign and later in the pursuit of Botha's commandos during the guerrilla phase.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Kaffrarian Rifles, a Cape Colony frontier regiment, served in the eastern Cape and later pursued Boer commandos including Scheepers and Lotter in the midlands.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Prince of Wales' Light Horse, a South African volunteer unit, served in the OFC and Transvaal guerrilla phases, conducting column operations against Boer commandos.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Rand Rifles, raised from Witwatersrand mine workers and residents after the British occupation of Johannesburg in May 1900, provided garrison and patrol duties in the Transvaal.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: French's cavalry division of 5,000 horses made a dramatic 35-mile ride on 15 February 1900, breaking through the Boer lines at Abon's Dam and entering Kimberley to relieve the siege.
source: Handbook ch.IX p.203

description: Roberts encircled Cronje's 4,000-man laager on the Modder River 18 Feb 1900. After Kitchener's costly frontal assault on 18 Feb (Paardeberg Drift), the Boers were starved into surrender on 27 Feb – Majuba Day – opening the road to Bloemfontein.
source: Conan Doyle ch.16; Handbook ch.XII p.224

description: Scottish Horse, raised by the Marquess of Tullibardine in early 1900, served extensively in the Transvaal and Orange River Colony guerrilla campaigns, playing a key role at Nooitgedacht follow-up and Rooiwal.
source: Conan Doyle ch.33; Handbook ch.XV

description: Roberts' Horse, an irregular mounted unit raised in 1900 and named in honour of the Field Marshal, served in the ORC and Transvaal during the guerrilla phase.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Colonel Plumer marched south from Bulawayo with approximately 900 men in April 1900, was checked at Ramathlabama on 11 May, but joined Mahon's column to relieve Mafeking on 17 May 1900.
source: Handbook ch.VIII p.189

description: The Railway Pioneer Regiment was responsible for repairing and operating the railway network across the OFS and Transvaal as Roberts's army advanced; they rebuilt the line to Bloemfontein and then to Pretoria under frequent Boer attack.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook ch.XII

description: Colonel Mahon's flying column of 1,000 men left Kimberley on 4 May 1900 and covered 250 miles in 13 days through Boer-held territory, joining Plumer's column to relieve Mafeking on 17 May 1900.
source: Handbook ch.VIII p.190

description: On 17 May 1900 Mahon and Plumer's combined force broke through the Boer besieging lines and entered Mafeking, ending the 217-day siege to widespread celebration throughout the British Empire.
source: Handbook ch.VIII p.191; Conan Doyle ch.23

description: Kitchener's Horse, raised mid-1900 as a mobile irregular unit, served in the guerrilla phase extensively in the OFS and Transvaal as a pursuit and intelligence force.
source: angloboerwar.com unit registry

description: Steinaecker's Horse, an irregular mounted unit, patrolled the Lowveld and the border with Portuguese East Africa in 1900-01 to intercept Boer communications and supplies from Mozambique.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook

description: Damant's Horse served in the OFS guerrilla phase in 1901, and was nearly destroyed by Wessels' commando using British uniforms as a decoy at Tafelkop in December 1901.
source: Conan Doyle ch.36

description: Strathcona's Horse, raised and equipped by Lord Strathcona, arrived mid-1900 and served in the Transvaal guerrilla phase, noted for its horsemanship and scouting ability.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook

description: A mixed force of 500 Australian, Rhodesian and other colonial troops under Lieutenant Hore withstood a siege by De la Rey's 2,000 Boers at Elandsrivier from 4-15 August 1900; their resistance became celebrated as a feat of colonial arms.
source: Conan Doyle ch.28; Handbook ch.XIV

description: The South African Constabulary, raised by Baden-Powell from January 1901, garrisoned the blockhouse lines across the Transvaal and Orange River Colony during the guerrilla phase, with a strength of approximately 10,000 men.
source: Handbook ch.XVII; Conan Doyle ch.37

description: The Bush Veldt Carbineers operated in the northern Transvaal from early 1901; the unit became notorious for summary executions of Boer prisoners â€” 'Breaker' Morant and Handcock were court-martialled and shot in February 1902.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Highland Brigade (Black Watch, Seaforth Highlanders, HLI, A&SH) under Major-General Wauchope arrived at Cape Town in late November 1899 and joined Methuen's 1st Division for the advance on Kimberley.
source: Conan Doyle ch.13; Handbook ch.IX

description: At Magersfontein on 11 December 1899 the Highland Brigade advanced in massed night formation and was caught in open ground at dawn by Boer rifles; Major-General Wauchope was killed and the brigade suffered 750 casualties before withdrawing.
source: Conan Doyle ch.13; Handbook ch.IX p.199

description: The Guards Brigade (Grenadier, Coldstream, Scots Guards) under Methuen fought at Belmont and Modder River in November 1899 before the check at Magersfontein; they subsequently garrisoned the line of communications in the OFS.
source: Conan Doyle ch.13

description: The Irish Guards, newly raised in 1900, arrived in South Africa and served in the OFS during the advance phase, providing garrison duties and line-of-communications protection.
source: angloboerwar.com unit registry

description: The 1st Natal Brigade under Penn Symons concentrated at Dundee in October 1899; the Royal Dublin Fusiliers, Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers, and Royal Irish Fusiliers were heavily engaged at Talana Hill on 20 October before the force retreated to Ladysmith.
source: Conan Doyle ch.6; Handbook ch.IV

description: The Devonshire Regiment, Gordon Highlanders, and Manchester Regiment formed part of White's Ladysmith garrison; the Gordons and Devons fought at Elandslaagte on 21 October 1899 and were besieged in Ladysmith from November 1899 to February 1900.
source: Conan Doyle ch.7; Handbook ch.V

description: The King's Royal Rifle Corps and Rifle Brigade were among the regiments besieged in Ladysmith from November 1899 to February 1900 as part of White's garrison.
source: Handbook ch.V

description: The Leicester Regiment, King's Liverpool Regiment, and Gloucestershire Regiment formed part of White's Ladysmith garrison during the siege from November 1899 to February 1900.
source: Handbook ch.V

description: The Royal Fusiliers, East Surrey Regiment, and West Yorkshire Regiment arrived in Natal as part of Buller's reinforcing divisions and served in the Tugela operations from December 1899 to February 1900.
source: Handbook ch.X; Conan Doyle ch.11

description: The Connaught Rangers and further battalions of the Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers served in Buller's Natal Army during the Tugela campaign and the advance into the Transvaal via Laing's Nek.
source: Handbook ch.XIII

description: Buller and Warren seized Spion Kop summit 23-24 Jan 1900 but the crest was dominated by Boer fire from surrounding heights. Thorneycroft held through the night then abandoned the summit. British ~1,700 casualties; a defining Black Week failure.
source: Conan Doyle ch.12; Handbook ch.XI

description: The Lincolnshire Regiment and Royal Scots Fusiliers formed part of the 6th Division that advanced with Roberts from the Modder River to Bloemfontein and Pretoria in early 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The Norfolk Regiment, South Lancashire Regiment, and Cheshire Regiment served in the 7th Division of Roberts's main army during the advance through the OFS to Pretoria in 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The Worcestershire Regiment, Durham Light Infantry, and East Lancashire Regiment formed part of the 8th Division that advanced under Roberts from the Orange River to Bloemfontein and beyond in early 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The Essex Regiment, Royal Warwickshire Regiment, and Welsh Regiment served in the 9th Division that crossed the Orange River with Roberts and fought through the OFS in 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The Royal Berkshire Regiment, East Yorkshire Regiment, and Yorkshire Regiment served in the 11th Division that formed part of Roberts's main army advancing from the Orange River Colony to the Transvaal.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The Somerset Light Infantry and Northamptonshire Regiment arrived in South Africa with Roberts's reinforcements and served in the OFS advance from February 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The Oxfordshire Light Infantry and Wiltshire Regiment served in Roberts's main army during the advance through the OFS and into the Transvaal in 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The East Kent Regiment (Buffs) and Hampshire Regiment served with Roberts's main army in the OFS and Transvaal campaigns of 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The North Staffordshire Regiment and York and Lancaster Regiment served in Roberts's columns advancing through the OFS and into the Transvaal in 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The South Wales Borderers and South Staffordshire Regiment served with Roberts's main army during the advance from the Orange River to Pretoria in 1900.
source: Handbook ch.XII

description: The Loyal North Lancashire Regiment garrisoned Kimberley and joined Methuen's force; the Northumberland Fusiliers served in the western Transvaal and suffered heavily at Nooitgedacht in December 1900.
source: Conan Doyle ch.31

description: The Cameron Highlanders, Cameronians (Scottish Rifles), and Bedfordshire Regiment served in the Cape Colony theatre during 1900-01, providing garrisons and column escorts as the guerrilla campaign spread southward.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook ch.XIV

description: The King's Own Scottish Borderers and Middlesex Regiment served as part of the Cape Colony garrison and column forces during the guerrilla phase 1900-02.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Royal West Surrey Regiment (Queen's) served in the Cape Colony during the guerrilla phase, providing garrison and column duties in the midland districts.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Royal West Kent Regiment served in the Cape Colony and later the ORC during the guerrilla phase 1900-02.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Royal Irish Rifles served in South Africa during 1900-01, operating in the Natal and Transvaal theatres during the guerrilla phase.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Royal Sussex Regiment served in the Transvaal during the guerrilla phase as part of Bruce Hamilton's 21st Brigade, participating in column drives.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 1st Royal Dragoons served in Roberts's main army during the advance from the Orange River to Pretoria in 1900, providing cavalry screen and pursuit roles.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook ch.XII

description: The Queen's Bays (2nd Dragoon Guards) served in Methuen's and French's cavalry division on the western front, participating in the relief of Kimberley.
source: Conan Doyle ch.14; angloboerwar.com

description: The 5th Dragoon Guards served in the Transvaal during the advance phase and guerrilla period of 1900-01.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 6th Dragoon Guards (Carabiniers) served in the Transvaal during the advance and guerrilla phases of 1900-01.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 10th Hussars formed part of the cavalry attached to White's Natal force and were besieged in Ladysmith from November 1899 to February 1900.
source: Handbook ch.V; angloboerwar.com

description: The 13th Hussars served in Roberts's cavalry force during the advance through the OFS and into the Transvaal in 1900.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 14th King's Hussars served in the Transvaal during the advance and guerrilla phases of 1900-02.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 15th Hussars served with Roberts's cavalry in the advance from the Modder River to Pretoria in 1900.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 18th Hussars served in Natal from the opening of the campaign and were engaged at Talana Hill on 20 October 1899, subsequently joining the Ladysmith garrison.
source: Conan Doyle ch.6; Handbook ch.IV

description: The 19th Hussars served with Roberts's cavalry division during the advance through the OFS and Transvaal in 1900.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 5th Royal Irish Lancers served in Natal from the opening of the campaign and formed part of the Ladysmith cavalry during the siege of November 1899 - February 1900.
source: Handbook ch.V; angloboerwar.com

description: The 9th Lancers served in French's cavalry under Methuen on the western front, participating in the advance to Kimberley; French's famous cavalry dash to relieve Kimberley in February 1900 included the regiment.
source: Conan Doyle ch.14

description: The 12th Royal Lancers served in Roberts's main cavalry force during the advance from the Orange River to Pretoria in 1900.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 16th Queen's Lancers served in the Transvaal during the advance and guerrilla phases of 1900-01.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The 17th Lancers served in Roberts's cavalry and in column drives during the guerrilla phase in the Transvaal.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Imperial Light Horse, raised in Natal from Uitlander refugees in 1899, served throughout the siege of Ladysmith and subsequently in the Natal advance, earning distinction at Elandslaagte on 21 October 1899.
source: Conan Doyle ch.7; Handbook ch.IV

description: The Natal Carbineers, an established colonial unit, served in the Ladysmith garrison throughout the siege and subsequently in the advance through Natal and the Transvaal.
source: Handbook ch.V

description: The Royal Canadian Dragoons (1st Canadian Mounted Rifles) served in the Transvaal during 1900, earning distinction at Leliefontein on 7 November 1900 where three Victoria Crosses were won.
source: angloboerwar.com; Conan Doyle ch.31

description: The 2nd Canadian Mounted Rifles served in the Transvaal in 1900 during the advance and early guerrilla phases.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Lord Strathcona's Horse, raised in Canada, served in the Transvaal during 1900, participating in column operations against the guerrilla commandos.
source: angloboerwar.com
note: date_start corrected from 1900-01-01 to 1900-06-05: Strathcona's Horse did not arrive in South Africa until April 1900; Pretoria fell Jun 5 1900 — Jan 1 placement was anachronistic

description: The New Zealand Mounted Rifles contingents (1st-5th) served in the Transvaal and ORC during 1900-01, noted for scouting and mobility in column operations.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Australian Horse contingents served in South Africa from early 1900, operating in the OFS and Transvaal during the advance and guerrilla phases.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: De Wet's commando struck the British water convoy at Sannaspos (Koorn Spruit) on 31 March 1900, capturing 117 wagons and 7 guns in the first major guerrilla coup of the war.
source: Conan Doyle ch.17; Handbook ch.XV

description: General De la Rey's commando operated in the western Transvaal Magaliesberg region from mid-1900 through the guerrilla phase, ambushing several columns and capturing Lord Methuen at Tweebosch in March 1902.
source: Conan Doyle ch.34; Handbook ch.XVIII

description: Botha's commando struck Benson's column at Bakenlaagte on 30 October 1901, inflicting 235 casualties including Benson himself, in one of the most effective guerrilla actions of the war.
source: Conan Doyle ch.34

description: Erasmus's commando formed part of the Boer forces at the battle of Talana Hill on 20 October 1899, threatening Penn Symons's flank during the engagement.
source: Conan Doyle ch.6

description: The Harrismith Commando operated in the northeastern OFS during the guerrilla phase, raiding into Natal and skirmishing with British garrisons.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Haasbroek's commando operated in the eastern OFS during 1901, conducting raids toward the Cape border.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Cape rebel commandos around Colesberg joined the Boer forces after the invasion of the Cape Colony in late 1899, skirmishing with French's cavalry until pushed back across the Orange River in March 1900.
source: Conan Doyle ch.9

description: Cape rebel commandos based on the Burghersdorp district rose during Smuts's and other commando raids into the north-eastern Cape, 1901-02.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: German volunteers organized as a foreign corps served with the Transvaal forces from the outbreak of war; the corps numbered several hundred men and served mainly in the eastern Transvaal.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: Dutch (Hollander) volunteers formed a corps in Transvaal service from the declaration of war, reflecting strong Dutch sympathy for the Boer cause; they served in the eastern Transvaal and Natal fronts.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Irish-American Brigade under Major John MacBride served with the Transvaal forces in Natal from late 1899, fighting at Colenso and later operations before disbanding in 1900.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Johannesburg (ZAR) Police served as a paramilitary unit from the outbreak of war, providing garrison and field duties in the Transvaal before the fall of Johannesburg in May 1900.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Orange Free State Artillery mobilized at Bloemfontein in October 1899 and served on all fronts, notably at Magersfontein and during the defence of the OFS capital.
source: angloboerwar.com; Handbook ch.IX

description: The Cape Town Highlanders, a colonial volunteer unit, served in the Cape Colony garrison and column operations during the guerrilla phase 1900-02.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Cape Garrison Artillery manned coastal and town defences at Cape Town from the outbreak of war, also providing field detachments for column service.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Border Horse, raised in the eastern Cape, served in column operations along the Cape-OFS border during the guerrilla phase 1900-02.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Border Mounted Rifles, a long-established eastern Cape colonial unit, served throughout the war providing mounted infantry for eastern Cape defence and column operations.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: A cyclist company raised in the Cape Colony for rapid courier and reconnaissance duties during the guerrilla phase.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Cape Medical Staff Corps provided medical support to colonial and British forces in the Cape Colony throughout the war.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Cape Railway Sharpshooters protected the railway lines in the Cape Colony from Boer sabotage and commando raids during 1900-02.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The East London Rifles, a Cape colonial volunteer corps, served in the eastern Cape and midlands during the guerrilla phase.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Kaffrarian Rifles (Mounted) were one of the oldest colonial units in the Cape Colony, serving in the eastern Cape throughout the war.
source: angloboerwar.com

description: The Queenstown Mounted Rifles served in the midland Cape Colony from the outbreak of war, defending against Boer invasions and conducting column operations.
source: angloboerwar.com
